name: Marina Beach
description: The world's second longest urban beach. Perfect for a breezy walk, street food binge, or just watching the waves with friends.

name: Elliot's Beach
description: A quieter, cleaner beach compared to Marina. Great for sitting alone, reading, or a peaceful evening stroll.

name: Amethyst Café
description: A heritage bungalow turned café with lush garden seating. Perfect for slow mornings, journaling, or a date.

name: Government Museum Chennai
description: India's second-oldest museum with ancient bronzes, Roman coins, and anthropology galleries.

name: Murugan Idli Shop
description: World-famous soft idlis with a dozen chutney varieties. If you're in Chennai and haven't been here, have you even been to Chennai?

name: Saravanaa Bhavan
description: The legendary Chennai-born South Indian chain. Crispy dosas, hot filter coffee, and a warm familiar feeling.

name: Birriyanis & More
description: Famous for their Ambur-style biryani and portions that make you regret nothing.

name: Cafe Ekdum
description: A quirky, vibrant café known for its biryanis and playful decor. Great vibes for a casual group meal.

name: Surfing at Covelong Point
description: One of South India's best surfing spots. Covelong Point Surf School offers lessons for beginners.

name: DakshinaChitra Heritage Museum
description: A living museum of South Indian art, architecture, and traditions.

name: Guindy National Park
description: A rare urban national park inside Chennai with blackbucks, spotted deer, and dense forest.

name: Cholamandal Artists Village
description: Asia's largest community of visual artists. Browse studios, buy original art, and walk through sculpture gardens.

name: Pottery Class at Studio Ochre
description: Learn to throw clay on a wheel in a peaceful studio. Incredibly meditative and satisfying.

name: Mylapore Cultural Walk
description: Wander through Mylapore's flower market, Brahmin coffee shops, kolam-decorated streets.

name: Kapaleeshwarar Temple
description: A stunning 7th-century Dravidian temple in the heart of Mylapore.

name: The Brew Room
description: One of Chennai's best specialty coffee spots. Clean, minimal, and laptop-friendly.

name: Higginbothams Bookstore
description: India's oldest bookstore, established in 1844 on Mount Road.

name: Broken Tusk Brewery
description: Chennai's popular microbrewery with in-house craft beers, live music weekends, and good food.

name: Pondicherry Promenade Beach
description: A colonial-era beachside promenade lined with colorful French villas. Best sunrise spot in South India.

name: Pondicherry White Town Walk
description: Wander through yellow and pastel-painted French colonial streets with bougainvillea overhanging.

name: Cafe des Arts Pondicherry
description: A charming French-style café in the heart of White Town. Croissants, filter coffee, and art on the walls.

name: Auroville
description: An experimental international township built around the Matrimandir. A place for meditation, silence, and clarity.

name: Karuvadikuppam Mangrove Walk
description: Kayak or walk through dense mangroves near Pondicherry. A quiet, almost surreal experience.

name: Le Cafe Pondicherry
description: A government-run sea-facing café on the promenade. Best spot for coffee while watching the waves at sunset.

name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
description: One of India's most magnificent temples — 14 towering gopurams with 33,000 sculptures.

name: Murugan Idli Shop Madurai
description: The Madurai branch of the legendary idli shop. Same fluffy idlis, dozens of chutneys.

name: Thirumalai Nayakkar Palace
description: A 17th-century palace with stunning Dravidian-Mughal architecture. The light-and-sound show in the evening is unforgettable.

name: Vandiyur Mariamman Teppakulam
description: A massive temple tank with a central island shrine. Boating available and breathtakingly peaceful at sunset.

name: Madurai Street Food Walk
description: Madurai is a foodie paradise. Try jigarthanda, parotta-salna, and mutton kari at the legendary street stalls near the temple.

name: Ooty Botanical Garden
description: A 22-hectare botanical garden with 650+ plant species in the Nilgiri hills.

name: Nilgiri Mountain Railway
description: Board the iconic UNESCO-listed toy train. Chug through 16 tunnels, 208 curves, and emerald tea estates.

name: Hike to Doddabetta Peak
description: The highest peak in the Nilgiri hills at 2637m. Breathtaking views above the cloud line.

name: Ooty Lake Boating
description: A picturesque artificial lake surrounded by eucalyptus trees. Pedal boats and row boats available.

name: Ooty Tea Factory Visit
description: Tour a working tea factory, learn how Nilgiri tea is processed, and sample fresh brews.

name: Mudumalai National Park
description: A wildlife sanctuary at the confluence of Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Kerala. Spot elephants, gaurs, and tigers.

name: Kodaikanal Lake
description: A star-shaped lake in the Palani hills surrounded by pine trees and mist.

name: Coaker's Walk Kodaikanal
description: A scenic 1km pedestrian path on the edge of a cliff. On clear days you can see the plains 2000m below.

name: Trekking to Dolphin's Nose
description: A moderate 8km trek to a rock formation overlooking the Palani valley. Misty, dramatic, and rewarding.

name: Cafe Cariappa Kodaikanal
description: A cozy hillside café with homemade food, great coffee, and misty views. Perfect for a slow rainy afternoon.

name: Pamban Bridge
description: Cross the iconic Pamban Bridge over the Palk Strait to reach the sacred island of Rameswaram.

name: Ramanathaswamy Temple
description: One of the 12 Jyotirlinga temples with the longest temple corridor in India at 1220 meters.

name: Kanyakumari Sunrise Point
description: The southernmost tip of India where the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and Indian Ocean meet.

name: Vivekananda Rock Memorial
description: A memorial built on a rock island where Swami Vivekananda meditated. Take a ferry and feel the sea breeze.

name: Brihadeeswarar Temple
description: A 1000-year-old Chola masterpiece and UNESCO World Heritage Site. The 66m vimana was built without scaffolding.

name: Thanjavur Art Gallery
description: Home to an exceptional collection of Chola bronze sculptures and Tanjore paintings.

name: Tanjore Plate Restaurant
description: Authentic Thanjavur cuisine — rich, aromatic curries, appam, and the famous Kavuni Arisi (black rice pudding).

name: Coimbatore Botanical Garden
description: A well-maintained botanical garden in the heart of Coimbatore with hundreds of plant species.

name: Marudhamalai Temple Trek
description: Trek up the Marudhamalai hills to a Murugan temple with panoramic views of Coimbatore city.

name: Coimbatore Street Food at Town Hall
description: Coimbatore's legendary street food scene around Town Hall. Kothu parotta, salna, and fresh juices.

name: Isha Yoga Center
description: The Isha Yoga Center at the foothills of Velliangiri with the stunning Dhyanalinga meditation space.

name: Cafe Amaretto Coimbatore
description: One of Coimbatore's most loved specialty coffee cafés. Great desserts and a warm atmosphere.

name: Rockfort Temple
description: A 7th-century temple perched on a 83m rock. Climb 417 steps carved into the rock for a stunning panoramic view.

name: Srirangam Temple
description: The largest functioning Hindu temple in the world — a self-contained temple town on an island.

name: Trichy Street Food at Chinna Kadai
description: Trichy's beloved street food hub. Try the local favourites — kari dosa, idiyappam, and fresh coconut chutney.

name: Vellore Fort
description: A 16th-century granite fort surrounded by a moat with crocodiles. One of the best preserved forts in South India.

name: Golden Temple Vellore
description: The Sripuram Golden Temple entirely covered in gold. Stunning at night when illuminated.

name: Vellore Street Food
description: Vellore's vibrant street food scene near the fort area. Biryani, shawarma, and fresh juices.

name: Salem Steel Plant Museum
description: A unique museum inside one of India's largest steel plants. Fascinating industrial heritage.

name: Yercaud Hills Trek
description: Trek through the Shevaroy Hills near Yercaud. Coffee estates, orchards, and misty valleys.

name: Salem Biryani at Buhari
description: Salem is famous for its unique style of biryani. Buhari is the legendary spot to experience it.

name: Gateway of India
description: The iconic 26-meter arch monument overlooking the Arabian Sea. The gateway to India, both literally and historically.

name: Marine Drive Mumbai
description: The stunning 3.6km coastal road along the Arabian Sea. Iconic at night when it resembles a queen's necklace.

name: Vada Pav at Kirti College
description: Mumbai's soul food — vada pav. The stalls near Kirti College serve the most authentic ones. Under ₹20 a piece.

name: Dharavi Street Food Walk
description: A guided food walk through Asia's largest slum turned cultural hub. Authentic local food at unbeatable prices.

name: Sanjay Gandhi National Park
description: A national park inside Mumbai city with leopards, crocodiles, and ancient Buddhist caves. Unbelievably wild for a metropolis.

name: Cafe Mondegar Mumbai
description: A legendary Mumbai café near Colaba with Mario Miranda murals, a vintage jukebox, and great food.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Museum
description: One of India's finest museums with Mughal miniatures, Buddhist sculptures, and natural history exhibits.

name: Cubbon Park
description: A 300-acre green lung in the heart of Bangalore with colonial buildings, fountains, and shaded walking paths.

name: MTR Bangalore
description: Mavalli Tiffin Rooms — a Bangalore institution since 1924. The masala dosa here is pure legend.

name: Nandi Hills Sunrise Trek
description: Drive or cycle up Nandi Hills before sunrise. The view of clouds below you at dawn is absolutely magical.

name: Bangalore Brew Works
description: One of Bangalore's top craft breweries with rotating seasonal beers, great food, and a lively atmosphere.

name: National Gallery of Modern Art Bangalore
description: One of India's finest modern art collections housed in a beautiful colonial building.

name: Toit Brewpub Bangalore
description: Bangalore's most iconic brewpub. House-brewed beers, great pizza, and a buzzing Indiranagar crowd.

name: Lalbagh Botanical Garden
description: A 240-acre botanical garden with a 19th-century glass house, a 3000-million-year-old rock, and stunning flower shows.

name: India Gate Delhi
description: India's iconic 42-meter war memorial on Rajpath. Glorious at dusk when lit up against the evening sky.

name: Paranthe Wali Gali Delhi
description: A legendary narrow lane in Chandni Chowk serving 150+ years of stuffed parathas. Pure Delhi soul food.

name: Humayun's Tomb
description: The first garden-tomb on the Indian subcontinent and the precursor to the Taj Mahal. Stunning Mughal architecture.

name: Lodhi Garden
description: A stunning 90-acre park with 15th-century Lodhi dynasty tombs. Delhi's most beautiful walking spot.

name: Karim's Delhi
description: Since 1913, Karim's near Jama Masjid has served the most legendary Mughlai food in India. The nihari is life-changing.

name: National Museum Delhi
description: India's largest museum with 200,000 artefacts spanning 5000 years of history including Indus Valley seals.

name: Hauz Khas Village
description: A medieval reservoir turned hipster village with boutique cafés, art galleries, and a stunning 14th-century complex.

name: Charminar
description: The iconic 16th-century mosque and monument at the heart of Hyderabad's Old City. Stunning when lit up at night.

name: Hyderabadi Biryani at Paradise
description: Paradise Restaurant — the home of authentic Hyderabadi dum biryani since 1953. The gold standard of biryani.

name: Hussain Sagar Lake
description: A stunning heart-shaped lake with a massive Buddha statue on a rock island. Ferry rides at sunset are magical.

name: Salar Jung Museum
description: One of the world's largest one-man collections. 43,000 artefacts including the famous Veiled Rebecca marble sculpture.

name: Golconda Fort
description: A magnificent 16th-century fort with an acoustic system so clever, a clap at the base can be heard 1km away at the top.

name: Irani Chai at Nimrah Cafe
description: A 1930s Irani café near Charminar. The Irani chai and Osmania biscuits are a Hyderabad institution.

name: Victoria Memorial
description: A grand white marble monument built in memory of Queen Victoria. The museum inside holds 25,000 colonial artefacts.

name: Kolkata Street Food Walk
description: Kolkata's street food is legendary. Puchka, kathi rolls, jhal muri, and mishti doi — a foodie's pilgrimage.

name: College Street Coffee House
description: The legendary Indian Coffee House on College Street — 70 years of intellectuals, writers, and students over coffee.

name: Howrah Bridge
description: The iconic cantilever bridge over the Hooghly River. One of the busiest bridges in the world — 100,000 vehicles daily.

name: Science City Kolkata
description: Asia's largest science museum with a space theatre, evolution park, and hundreds of interactive exhibits.

name: Amer Fort Jaipur
description: A stunning 16th-century fort on a hilltop overlooking Maota Lake. Intricate mirror work and massive courtyards.

name: Jaipur Street Food at Johari Bazaar
description: Jaipur's legendary bazaar food. Dal baati churma, pyaaz kachori, and the famous Lassiwala's thick cold lassi.

name: Hawa Mahal
description: The iconic Palace of Winds — 953 small windows designed so royal ladies could watch street life unseen.

name: Nahargarh Fort Jaipur
description: A fort perched on the Aravalli hills with breathtaking views of Jaipur city. The sunset here is unforgettable.

name: Goa Beaches
description: Goa's famous beaches — Baga, Calangute, Anjuna. Water sports, shack food, sunset cocktails, and pure vibes.

name: Goa Spice Plantation
description: Walk through lush spice gardens — cardamom, pepper, vanilla, and cloves. Includes a traditional Goan lunch.

name: Goan Fish Curry at Fisherman's Wharf
description: Authentic Goan fish curry and rice at a riverside restaurant. Recheado pomfret, prawn balchao, and sol kadhi.

name: Old Goa Churches
description: The Basilica of Bom Jesus and Se Cathedral — UNESCO World Heritage churches with St. Francis Xavier's tomb.

name: Kochi Fort Walk
description: Walk through Fort Kochi's colonial streets with Dutch, Portuguese, and British architecture, street art, and cafés.

name: Kerala Backwater Houseboat
description: Drift through Kerala's legendary backwaters on a traditional rice boat houseboat. The most serene experience in India.

name: Kochi Seafood at Grandma's
description: Authentic Kerala home cooking. Fish molee with appam, karimeen pollichathu, and fresh coconut chutney.

name: Mattancherry Spice Market
description: Walk through 500-year-old spice warehouses. The air is thick with pepper, cardamom, and cinnamon.

name: Cherai Beach Kochi
description: A pristine beach 35km from Kochi where a backwater lagoon meets the sea. Spot dolphins if you're lucky.

name: Mahabalipuram Shore Temple
description: A UNESCO World Heritage site — 7th-century Pallava stone temples on the shores of the Bay of Bengal.

name: Chettinad Heritage Village
description: Explore the opulent mansions of the Chettinad region — Italian marble floors, Belgian glass, and teak wood carvings.

name: Chettinad Restaurant Karaikudi
description: Authentic Chettinad cuisine — fiery chicken curry, kara kuzhambu, and paniyaram. One of India's most complex cuisines.

name: Vedanthangal Bird Sanctuary
description: India's oldest bird sanctuary with thousands of migratory birds from Australia and Siberia.

name: Stargazing at Kavalur Observatory
description: India's largest optical telescope in the Javadi Hills. A humbling look at the universe on a clear night.
